name: La Cierva Reservoir and Fuente Caputa Loop
name_original: Embalse de la Cierva y Poza de Fuente Caputa — vuelta desde Mula por el Castillo de los Vélez
description: Cycle 16.5 miles (26.6 km) past La Cierva Reservoir, Poza de Fuente Caputa, and Castillo de los Vélez on this moderate route.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Mula offers a diverse landscape, characterized by the Mula river valley, expansive pine forests, and unique "badlands" formations. The region features a blend of natural beauty and historical routes, including the Vía Verde del Noroeste. Cyclists can explore varied terrains, from lush fruit orchards to more arid, desert-like areas. The area provides well-maintained infrastructure suitable for touring cyclists of different experience levels. Best touring cycling routes around Mula The most popular touring cycling route is La Cierva Reservoir and Fuente Caputa Loop, a 16.5 miles (26.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route explores the scenic reservoir and the natural feature of Fuente Caputa. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Luz Reservoir – Mula River Viaduct loop from Mula, a moderate 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This route follows the Mula River, offering views of historical viaducts and the surrounding green landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Green land in sight – Espubinc Visitor Center loop from Los Ojos, a 28.8 miles (46.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards the Espubinc Visitor Center, often completed in about 4 hours. Touring cycling around Mula is defined by the Vía Verde del Noroeste, the Mula river valley, and the Sierra Espuña regional park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Mula are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mula's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mula?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Mula, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of moderate and difficult trails, with a few easier options as well.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Mula?
A: Mula offers a remarkably diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You can expect to traverse the lush Mula river valley, expansive pine forests, and unique 'badlands' formations. Routes also feature fruit orchards, almond groves, and even desert-like areas near Los Baños de Mula, providing a dynamic and varied cycling experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Mula?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016686874/la-cierva-reservoir-and-fuente-caputa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cierva Reservoir and Fuente Caputa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.6 km route that explores scenic natural features, making it suitable for families with some cycling experience. The Vía Verde del Noroeste, a well-conditioned greenway, also offers sections that are generally flat and safe for family outings.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can discover several interesting natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5092026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Usero&lt;/a&gt; are picturesque spots, often incorporated into routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de Algeciras Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful views, and the historic structures along the Vía Verde del Noroeste, such as old railway viaducts and tunnels, add a unique historical dimension to your ride.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Mula?
A: October is a particularly popular month for cycling in Mula due to favorable weather conditions. Additionally, late winter and early spring are ideal for witnessing the beautiful almond blossom, with guided tours often available to enjoy the charming rural atmosphere.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Mula area?
A: Many routes around Mula are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore without retracing your steps. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10321214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Luz Reservoir – Mula River Viaduct loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 36.2 km circular path that follows the Mula River and offers views of historical viaducts.

Q: What is the Vía Verde del Noroeste and how does it relate to cycling in Mula?
A: The Vía Verde del Noroeste (Northwest Greenway) is a significant draw for touring cyclists in Mula. It's a 78-kilometer route built on an old railway line, connecting Murcia to Caravaca de la Cruz. A 21.4 km section from Mula to Bullas follows the Mula river basin, offering a well-conditioned and signposted path suitable for cyclists of all levels, showcasing diverse scenery and historical railway structures.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Mula?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mula are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from lush river valleys to unique badlands, and the well-maintained infrastructure, especially along the Vía Verde del Noroeste.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Mula offers several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green land in sight – Espubinc Visitor Center loop from Los Ojos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 46.4 km trail with significant elevation changes, leading through varied terrain towards the Espubinc Visitor Center.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of Sierra Espuña?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra Espuña regional park borders Mula and provides excellent scenic opportunities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sierra Espuña – Espubinc Visitor Center loop from El Mirador de Gebas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.9 km route specifically designed to offer stunning vistas of the Sierra Espuña landscape.

Q: What kind of historical sites can I encounter while cycling in Mula?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Mula's routes offer glimpses into its history. Along the Vía Verde del Noroeste, you can see old train stations, impressive viaducts, and tunnels. The historic Mula Castle is also a prominent landmark visible from various points, and the Hermitage of Santa Eulalia is another notable historical highlight.

Q: Are there routes that pass through unique landscapes like 'badlands'?
A: Yes, Mula is known for its peculiar natural scenery, including stunning desert-like areas and 'badlands' formations, particularly near Los Baños de Mula. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40341702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Mula – Chess and Lion Mural loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt; will take you through these distinctive landscapes, offering a truly unique cycling experience.
tips: The castle of Renaissance origin is located at the top of the town of Mula. From its walls, you can see the Murcian orchard and the mountains on the horizon.

The De La Cierva reservoir is located in a beautiful setting. We will find people hiking, biking, fishing or climbing. There is also a picnic area if we want to eat something while enjoying the views.

Located in the Sierra de Ricote and surrounded by extensions of pine forests and vertical limestone walls that make this a wonderful place, we find ourselves in the Poza de Fuente Caputa, whose name comes from Roman times and its meaning is "head of the water". » Without a doubt, one of the best pools in the Region of Murcia. In some section we will have to be attentive in case we have to get off the bike and push it for a few meters.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate, requiring good physical fitness. You should anticipate a mix of surfaces, as some sections may be unpaved or present difficulties for touring cycling, ranging from asphalt to gravel or dirt paths. Near the Castillo de los Vélez, there's a path with a quite steep slope that could be challenging.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is described as returning from Mula, implying Mula could be a good starting point. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, Mula, as a town, would offer parking options. It's advisable to check local parking facilities in Mula before your visit.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse and picturesque scenery. Highlights include the natural beauty around La Cierva Reservoir and Poza de Fuente Caputa, an oasis with natural pools. Expect a contrast of stunning desert landscapes with the greenish hues of the Mula river valley, fruit orchards, pine forests, and almond groves. You'll also get historical views of the Castillo de los Vélez and the town of Mula.

faqs.question: What are the main landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Key landmarks include the impressive Castillo de los Vélez, offering commanding views over Mula. You'll also pass by the beautiful La Cierva Reservoir and the unique oasis of Poza de Fuente Caputa, known for its natural pools and archaeological significance with Roman sites nearby.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn are generally the best times for cycling in Murcia. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. This allows for a more comfortable experience enjoying the varied landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route incorporate parts of the Vía Verde del Noroeste, a well-established cycling infrastructure known for its varied landscapes. It also touches upon the Camino del Apóstol, Camino del Pantano, and other local paths.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the natural areas are generally accessible, specific rules for dogs on a touring bicycle route can vary. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other cyclists. Be mindful of the terrain and distance, ensuring it's suitable for your dog's fitness level.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route, as it largely uses public roads and established greenways. However, always respect private property and any posted local regulations, especially around historical sites or protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or archaeological sites along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. Near La Cierva Reservoir, there are Roman sites, including a Roman villa (Los Villaricos), indicating ancient water usage. The Castillo de los Vélez itself is a significant historical landmark, and nearby paths might reveal glimpses of old Muslim walls and watchtowers.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat, drink, or find accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: As the route returns from Mula, the town itself offers various amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in Mula to refuel or stay overnight. It's a good idea to plan your stops, especially if you're looking for specific services.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead or return from the end point?
faqs.answer: Mula, being a town, likely has public transport connections. However, transporting bicycles on public transport can be restricted. It's recommended to check local bus or train services in Murcia for their bicycle policies if you plan to use public transport to access or leave the route.
